prompt: Create a collectible fine-art landscape photograph as if captured by a world-class National Geographic photographer: a high mountain overlook looking down into a secluded Chinese river valley at early morning in spring. The scene shows a misty village nestled among forested hills, with dark tiled-roof houses scattered through bright green fields and orchards full of pale pink and white blossoms. A narrow stream winds through the right side of the valley, while a broad river curves in the far left distance between layered blue mountains. Foreground includes rugged mossy limestone cliffs and dark pine branches framing the view from both sides, creating depth. Count the main visible landscape elements as 1 winding distant river, 1 narrow valley stream, 1 clustered village, many blossoming orchard trees, terraced green fields, layered mountain ridges, and low drifting fog banks. Use soft golden morning light breaking through cool blue haze, volumetric mist, realistic atmospheric perspective, crisp natural detail, deep greens, muted slate-blue mountains, no people, no animals, no modern roads, no power lines, no text. The mood should feel like an ancient Chinese poem discovered as a real place, serene, vast, humid, and sacred. Shot on a medium-format camera with a wide-angle lens, high dynamic range, natural color grading, ultra-realistic documentary photography. Optional source inspiration: an ancient Chinese description of a hidden peach-blossom valley in the mountains; season: early spring; time of day: misty sunrise; location style: remote Chinese mountain village; mood: serene and timeless.
